memory.summary: I really want to grab that quick chore win, but honestly, spending the whole afternoon hanging out with the cohort feels way better than stressing over this project.

desires: Stay in the flow with cohort_3 for another hour; the project deadline feels distant enough that hanging out now is worth more than starting early.
Get a quick dopamine hit from finishing that trivial chore so I can immediately jump back into the group chat without feeling guilty about ignoring them.
Not have to explain to the team why I'm behind on the project later; just hope it doesn't matter yet.

goals.description: Finish the trivial quick_chore immediately to get that dopamine hit and silence my guilt so I can jump back into the group chat with cohort_3.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference means I value the immediate emotional relief of a 'win' over the distant project deadline. Doing this first clears my head for socializing, which is my top priority (0.5 weight), while avoiding the friction of explaining why I'm distracted later.

goals.description: Hang out with cohort_3 for an hour; the term project feels far enough away that it doesn't matter yet.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my highest value. However, my avoidant conflict style makes me hesitate to start working now because I don't want to face the team or feel the stress of being behind. I'll defer work until after I've reconnected with them, hoping the deadline won't force a confrontation soon.

agent.last_reasoning: Need the instant dopamine hit to silence guilt before I can relax with cohort_3. Skipping this feels risky; I'll just power through it now so I don't owe anyone an explanation later.
